Here گفتی appears as the reporting verb (note: the provided lemma senses did not include the simple past ‘you said,’ so sense is marked -1). ببارید is past tense of باریدن “to fall/rain,” used metaphorically here. از چشم خون = ‘blood from (the) eye,’ with از = “from,” چشم = “eye,” خون = “blood.” The clause reports speech: ‘you said that blood rained from the eye.’
segments.s671-g59: منش را دگر کرد = ‘he changed his manner/character.’ منش = ‘manner/character,’ را is the object/definiteness marker attached to منش, دگر = ‘another/changed,’ and کرد is the past tense of کردن ‘to do/make’ (here ‘changed’). و پاسخ دگر = ‘and (gave) another answer’—پاسخ = ‘answer/reply,’ دگر = ‘another’ (parallel to منش). The structure is subject omitted (understood from context) + verb phrase + coordinating clause.
segments.s671-g60: توگفتی = ‘you said’ (perfect/simple past reporting verb). بپروین is a prepositional/dir. phrase meaning ‘toward Parvin’ (بـ = toward/at + پروین, here fused). برآورد is an old/perfective verb form of برآوردن meaning ‘raised/brought up’ (in this context ‘lifted’), followed by سر = ‘head.’ The literal order is reporting-verb + direction phrase + verb + object: ‘you said (that) he lifted his head toward Parvin.’
segments.s671-g61: بیامد هم اندر پی نره گور = ‘(he) also came/arrived in pursuit of the male onager.’ بیامد is past tense ‘came,’ هم = ‘also,’ اندر پی = ‘in pursuit of’ (اندر = ‘in/within,’ پی = ‘after/following’), نره = ‘male’ (specifying the beast), گور = ‘onager’ (wild ass). Word order is verb + focus particle + locative/pursuit phrase + noun phrase.
segments.s671-g62: سپهبد پس اندر همی‌راند بور = ‘the spahbed then/afterwards drove the (animal) in (inside), the بور (descriptive).’ سپهبد = title ‘spahbed’ (general). پس = ‘then/after,’ اندر = ‘in,’ همی‌راند is the imperfective/continuous past ‘was driving’ (همی- prefix marks ongoing/past habitual), and بور is an epithet meaning ‘fair-haired/pale’ describing the steed. The phrase places the action (driving) after the arrival.
segments.s671-g63: چنین تا ازان بیشه آمد برون = ‘thus/until from that thicket he came out.’ چنین = ‘thus/so,’ تا = ‘until,’ ازان = ‘from that,’ بیشه = ‘thicket/grove,’ آمد = past ‘came,’ برون = ‘out.’ The ordering places the temporal/result clause first: ‘thus, until he came out of that grove…’
segments.s671-g64: همی‌بود بهرام را رهنمون = ‘he continued to be Bahram’s guide.’ همی‌بود is an imperfective/past habitual ‘continued to be’ (همی- prefix + بود), بهرام = proper name ‘Bahram,’ را here marks the person affected (a dative/definite marker in this construction), and رهنمون = ‘guide/guidance.’ The construction is verbless/copular in sense: ‘(he) was a guide to Bahram’ with همی‌بود giving continuous past meaning.
segments.s671-g153: که introduces a relative clause ('which/that'). در بیشه گوری is a locative prepositional phrase ('in the grove of graves'), with در + noun. بود is the past copula 'was' placed before the predicate (poetic word order: verb before predicate), and رهنمای is the predicate noun 'a guide' (i.e. 'was a guide').
segments.s671-g154: میان بیابان is another locative phrase meaning 'in the midst/middle of the desert' (میان as 'middle'). بی‌بر modifies the following noun as an adjective meaning 'ill‑fated/desolate', and سرای is the head noun 'dwelling/house' (here 'desolate dwelling'). The adjective + noun cluster gives a descriptive location following the previous clause.
segments.s671-g155: برتخت زرین is a locative/possession phrase 'on the golden throne' (بر + تخت + adjective زرین). یکی تاجدار is an indefinite noun phrase 'a certain crowned one' or 'a crowned man' acting as the subject; classical/poetic Persian often places such nominal phrases after the locative phrase.
segments.s671-g156: پرستار is a substantive meaning 'caretaker/keeper' here. پیش اندرون is a locative/relational phrase meaning 'before/in front of the inner (quarters)' (پیش = 'before/in presence of', اندرون = 'inner/within'), and شاهوار is an attributive adjective 'regal/lofty' modifying اندرون. Together they describe his function/place: 'a keeper before the inner regal apartments.'
segments.s671-g157: بکردار is an adjective 'virtuous' describing the character/quality. خوابیست is a compound copular expression meaning 'is a dream' (خوابی + است → 'is dreamlike' or 'is a dream'), and این داستان is the demonstrative noun phrase 'this tale/story' (subject); word order is poetic, with the predicate cluster preceding the explicit demonstrative noun.
segments.s671-g158: که introduces a relative clause modifying 'this tale'. برخواند here is used in the narrative sense 'is/was recited' (classical epic present used for reported/continuous narration). از گفته باستان = 'from ancient reports/what is said of old' (از = 'from', گفته = 'saying/report', باستان = 'ancient'), so the clause means 'which is recited from ancient accounts.'
segments.s671-g159: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so'. گفت is the past verb 'said' (poetic past). موبد is the subject 'the mobed/priest'. بشاه جهان is the phrase 'to the king of the world' or vocative/title 'O king of the world' (بِـ- before شاه = 'to the king' / or direct address), so the whole segment reads 'Thus the priest said to the king of the world.'
segments.s671-g160: که introduces reported content ('that...'). آن گور is 'that tomb/grave' (demonstrative + noun). دیوی (here written as دیو + ی) functions as a genitive/adjectival form 'demonic/of a demon' ('a demon's' or 'demonic'). بود is the past copula 'was', and درنهان (در نهان) means 'in secret/hidden' — together conveying 'that that tomb was (the place of) a demon in hiding' or more naturally 'that the tomb concealed a demon.'
segments.s671-g25: This is a verbless prepositional noun phrase giving the location/state: 'بران' (an emphatic poetic locative = 'upon that/thereon') precedes the head noun phrase 'تخت فرشی' (literally 'throne-carpet' = 'a carpet on the throne'). The particle 'ز' is the poetic genitive/partitive 'of/from' linking 'دیبای روم' ('Roman brocade') to the preceding noun; word order is typical of classical Persian descriptive phrases where modifiers follow the noun.
segments.s671-g26: 'همه پیکرش' = 'its/its whole surface' — 'همه' here means 'whole/entire' and 'پیکرش' is 'پیکر' with the third-person possessive suffix '-ش' ('its/his body/surface'). The following nominal phrase 'گوهر و زر بوم' is a predicative/material description: 'گوهر و زر' (gems and gold) qualified by 'بوم' (here 'cloth/canvas'), so the whole phrase says the surface was of gems and gold (i.e., studded/covered with them).
segments.s671-g27: 'نشسته برو' is a fixed poetic locative expression meaning 'sat there/on it' (here 'نشسته' = 'seated/sitting' and 'برو' functions archaically as a locative/emphatic particle). It is followed by the prepositional phrase 'بر زنی تاجدار' = 'upon a crowned woman' (literally 'on a woman crown‑bearing'); note the poetic omission of the ezafe is common, and the whole segment names who is seated.
segments.s671-g28: This gives two parallel similes describing her appearance. 'به بالا چو سرو' = 'in height/upward (به بالا) like (چو) a cypress (سرو)' — 'به' here is adverbial ('in/by ...-ness') forming the simile. The conjunction 'و' links the second simile 'به رخ چون بهار' = 'and in face like spring' ('رخ' = face, 'چون' = like, 'بهار' = spring). Word order places the adverbial phrase before the simile noun as is normal in Persian poetic style.
segments.s671-g29: 'بر تخت زرین' = 'on the golden throne' where 'بر' is the preposition 'on/upon' and 'تخت زرین' is a noun plus adjective ('golden throne'). 'یکی زیرگاه' = 'a certain/lower seat' — 'یکی' here = 'a certain' or 'one (a particular)' and 'زیرگاه' literally 'place beneath' (i.e. a subordinate or lower seat). Together they locate the person mentioned next.
segments.s671-g30: 'نشسته برو' again = 'sat there/on it'. The phrase 'پهلوان سپاه' is a noun-noun apposition meaning 'the champion (پهلوان) of the army/host (سپاه)'; classical poetry often omits the ezafe, so the relation is understood as genitive/possessive ('champion belonging to the army').
segments.s671-g31: 'فراوان پرستنده' = 'many worshippers' where 'فراوان' quantifies 'پرستنده' (an agent noun 'worshipper/devotee'). 'بر گرد تخت' is a prepositional phrase 'around the throne' ('بر گرد' = 'around/about' + 'تخت' = 'throne'), so the segment describes the crowding of devotees around the throne.
segments.s671-g32: This is a compact descriptive string about the objects of worship: 'بتان' = 'idols' (the things being worshipped). 'پری روی' is a common compound meaning 'fairy-faced' (پری = fairy/beautiful, روی = face). 'بیدار بخت' are two adjectives in apposition: 'بیدار' = 'awake/alert' and 'بخت' = 'fortune/luck' used adjectivally (here 'fortunate' or 'favored by fate'); classical Persian often strings such adjectives without conjunctions.
segments.s671-g241: کنون is an adverb meaning 'now' placed at the sentence start to set time. دردمندیم is a first-person-plural predication formed from دردمند + -یم, literally 'we are sorrowful/afflicted'; adjective + copula is normal word order. اندرجهان (an older/poetic prepositional phrase) follows the predicate and specifies location: 'in the world.'
segments.s671-g242: بداننده is a dative/benefactive-like nominal 'the one who knows' or 'the knower' (here the addressee/recipient). گوییم is a first-person-plural verb 'we say / we tell' (classical/poetic present). یکسر is an adverb 'entirely/wholly' and نهان is an adjective/nominal 'hidden/secret' describing what is told; word order puts the recipient (بداننده) before the verb and the object/description after it.
segments.s671-g243: برفتیم is the past tense first-person-plural 'we went / we departed.' ز ایران is the prepositional phrase 'from Iran' (ز is the poetic form of از). چنین modifies the following noun phrase and means 'such/thus'; کینه‌خواه is a compound notion 'seeking vengeance / bent on grudge' (خواه here indicates the sense 'seeking' attached to کینه), so the whole clause says the subject left Iran with that resentful intent.
segments.s671-g244: بدین مایه is a prepositional/causal phrase 'by/with this cause/motive' (بدین = بِ + دین in the written segmentation but functions as the single adverbial 'by this'). لشکر is the noun 'army' and بفرمان شاه is a prepositional/agent phrase 'by the order of the king' (بفرمان = 'by the command of'). The whole segment states that, for this reason, an army was raised at the king's order.
segments.s671-g245: ازین بیش is a comparative phrase meaning 'beyond this / greater than this' (ازین = از + این, here written as two items); لشکر is the subject 'army.' نبیند is a negative present/future-like form 'does not / will not see' (ن‑ + بیند), and کسی in a negative clause functions as 'anyone' → here interpreted as 'no one.' Word order is emphatic: the comparative phrase begins the clause.
segments.s671-g246: وگر is the conditional 'and/if' (introducing a concession); چند is an indefinite 'a few / some'; ماند is the present/subjunctive or potential 'remain' ('if a few remain'); بگیتی is another locative 'in the world'; بسی is a predicative adverb/adjective 'enough / sufficient.' Together this yields a concessive conditional: 'but if some remain in the world, that will be enough.'
segments.s671-g247: چوپرموده is a poetic compound meaning roughly 'as if taught/fully instructed' (چو = 'as/when', پرموده = 'taught/filled'); گرد here is an adverb or verbal element meaning 'about/around' or 'went about.' با ساوه شاه is an instrumental/associative phrase 'with the king of Saveh.' The clause is a temporal/qualifying clause describing an action taken while accompanied by the Saveh king.
segments.s671-g248: اگر is the protasis 'if'; سوی ایران is the directional phrase 'toward Iran'; کشیدی سپاه is the verb phrase 'you drew/led the army' (کشیدی is second-person-past/conditional in classical usage meaning 'you moved/drew'); the whole segment presents a conditional continuation ('if you lead the army toward Iran').
segments.s671-g321: بدو گفت = 'he said to him': 'بدو' is the dative/enclitic form 'to him' placed before the verb. 'کین را' uses the object marker را to mark 'kīn' (vengeance/grudge) as the direct object. 'مپندار' is the negative imperative/prohibitive 'do not think/suppose' (m- prefix + pandar root). 'خرد' follows as the predicate noun 'wisdom.' The order is topical object + negative command: 'Do not think vengeance (to be) wisdom.'
segments.s671-g322: که introduces an explanatory clause ('for/that'). 'دیهیم را خرد' = 'the villager's wisdom' with را marking the theme/topic. 'نتوان' is the present inability 'cannot'; when followed by a simple verb it expresses inability. 'شمرد' is the lexical verb 'to count/reckon' (here in the sense 'be counted/reckoned'), so نتوان شمرد = 'cannot be counted.'
segments.s671-g323: چنین گفت = 'thus he said' (چنین = 'thus/so', گفت = 'said'). 'زان پس' = 'after that/thereupon' (زان = 'from that', پس = 'then/after'). بپیداگشسب is a fused verbal phrase naming the subject: the prefix بـ with پیدَا + گشسب (the name Gashasp) yields 'Gashasp appeared/was found' — an archaic compound used here to report his coming on the scene.
segments.s671-g324: که‌ای here introduces the following vocative/description (که = 'who/that', ای = vocative 'O'). The sequence 'تیغ‌زن شیر تازنده اسب' stacks epithets: تیغ‌زن 'sword-striker/swordsman', شیر 'lion' (used metaphorically), تازنده 'vigorous/charging' modifying اسب 'horse/steed'. Together they form an exclamatory address: 'O sword-striker, lion-rushing steed.'
segments.s671-g325: This is a pair of parallel interrogatives: 'چه بینی' = 'what do you see?' (چه = 'what', بینی = 2nd-person present 'you see'). 'چه گویی' = 'what do you say?' (parallel structure; گویی here is the 2nd-person present 'you say'). 'بدین' is the prepositional compound 'بـ' + 'دین' meaning 'about/with this' (i.e. 'regarding this'), followed by 'کار ما' = 'our affair/this matter'.
segments.s671-g326: بود is the past copula 'there was/it was'. 'گاه' = 'a time/occasion', so 'بود گاه' = 'there was a time'. 'شاهی' here means 'kingship' and 'سزاوار ما' = 'fitting/appropriate for us' (سزاوار takes the beneficiary/possessor after it). Word order is archaic but yields: 'There was a time when kingship was fitting for us.'
segments.s671-g327: Again, 'چنین گفت' = 'thus he said/spoke'. پیداگشسب is the verbalized/name cluster indicating that Gashasp was present/appeared or is the speaker (پیدا + گشسب), and 'سوار' is an appositive 'the rider/horseman'. The phrase functions to introduce Gashasp as 'Gashasp, the rider,' who then speaks.
segments.s671-g328: که‌ای introduces an address/exclamation (که = relative/that; ای = vocative 'O'). 'از یلان جهان' = 'of the heroes of the world' (از marking partitive/one of). 'یادگار' is used as 'remnant/memento' or 'memorial', here meaning someone who remains as the memory/vestige of past heroes: 'O remnant of the world's heroes.'
segments.s671-g161: This clause functions as a temporal/causal lead-in: چوبهرام را خواند = "(he) called/summoned Bahram." را is the definite-object marker attached to بهرام; خواند is the simple past of خواندن used here in the sense "to call, summon." The initial element is written together (چو‑بهرام) but functions as "when/when he" + object phrase in context — idiomatically rendered here as the event of summoning Bahram.
segments.s671-g162: پدید آمد is a light-verb construction meaning "appeared/arose" (پدید + آمد = 'came into view'). اندر دلش is the locative phrase "in his heart" (اندر = in/within, ش = his). کاستی is the noun "deficiency/shortcoming," the thing that 'appeared'.
segments.s671-g163: همان = "that same/that very." کاخ جادوستانی is a nominal phrase "palace of the sorcerers/enchanted palace." شناس here is used adjectivally/predicatively in the sense "knowing/recognizing" (i.e. he knew/recognized that palace). Word order places the identifying adjective after the noun phrase in the poetic register.
segments.s671-g164: بدان is composed of ب (directional/to) + دان, functioning together to mean "to that" or "as to that" (here introducing the specification). تخت جادو زنی is a genitive/compound: تخت = "throne," جادو + زنی = "magic‑woman" → "witch/enchanted woman." ناسپاس is a postpositive adjective "ungrateful/thankless," modifying the witch. The whole phrase = "to/that throne of the ungrateful witch."
segments.s671-g165: که introduces a relative clause: "which/that." بهرام را is the object with را (here the indirect object of the bestowal); آن سترگی is the thing bestowed — "that greatness/majesty." نمود is the past tense of نمودن used here as a transitive verb "showed/bestowed/caused to appear." The clause reads: "which bestowed upon Bahram that greatness."
segments.s671-g166: چنان is an intensifier "such/so" modifying the following noun phrase. تاج و تخت (here written وتخت) is the paired phrase "crown and throne," with بزرگی qualifying them as "of greatness" or "mighty." نمود repeats as the past verb "placed/produced/manifested." Together: "It thus placed on him crown and a great throne."
segments.s671-g167: چو برگشت ازو = "when he returned from it" (چو = when; برگشت = returned; ازو = from it/him). پرمنش is an adjective meaning "high‑minded / of lofty manners," and گشت is the past of گشتن used as a copular/auxiliary meaning "became." و مست = "and intoxicated/drunk (with pride)" — here و + مست is a poetic coordinate (مست = 'intoxicated'), though the provided sense list did not include this meaning, so the listed item is taken as the conjunction plus adjective 'مست' in context.
segments.s671-g168: چنان دان = "he considered it so/thus" (دان here = to consider/hold). که introduces the content clause: هرگز نیاید بدست = "that it would never come into (any) hand" — هرگز = never, نیاید is the present/subjunctive negative meaning "does not/will not come," and بدست = "into the hand / into someone's possession" (i.e. 'be obtained'). The sense: he thought it so secure that it would never be taken.
segments.s671-g121: بدو گفت بهرام: linear order here places the prepositional pronoun بدو ('to him') before the verb گفت ('said', past 3rd person). The speaker's name بهرام follows the verb (verb-subject order), and شاید is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps' introducing uncertainty; بدن ('the body') is the direct object placed before the verb phrase that follows (Persian allows objects to precede the verb).
segments.s671-g122: بنیک وببد رای is a coordinated noun phrase 'good and bad counsel/plan' (بنیک = 'good', وببد = 'and bad', رای = 'counsel/plan'). باید is the modal auxiliary 'must/ought', and زدن is the infinitive 'to strike/act' that completes باید (باید زدن = 'one must strike/act'). The object بدن from the previous segment functions as the patient of زدن (object-before-verb pattern).
segments.s671-g123: زیانی ('a loss') is the head noun; که introduces a relative clause ('that/which'). بودش is the past copula with the enclitic pronoun (-ش) meaning 'that was his' or 'that belonged to him.' همه ('all') modifies what follows, and باز داد is a compound where باز (back/again) + داد (gave) means 'gave back' or 'restored.' The sequence expresses 'he gave back all the loss that was his.'
segments.s671-g124: هم ('also') + از ('from') introduces the source: گنج خویشش ('his own treasure' — خویشش = خویش + enclitic = 'his own'). بسی is an adverbial quantifier 'much/plenty' and ساز here functions as 'means/devices' or 'wealthly provisions' (a poetic use). داد is the past verb 'gave.' Together: 'and from his own treasure he gave much.'
segments.s671-g125: بدو گفت again places the recipient 'to him' before the verb 'said.' زان پس ('thereafter' / 'from then on') sets the temporal frame, and که introduces the content of the admonition. تو ساز خویش = 'your instrument' (ساز = instrument/tune, خوی + ش = خویش 'your/your own'); word order is possessive noun phrase with enclitic possessor.
segments.s671-g126: بژرفی is an adverb meaning 'deeply/closely'; نگه‌دار is an imperative-form verb 'keep' or 'hold' (here a command to preserve/keep something near). و = 'and'. مگریز is a negative or prohibitive form meaning 'do not flee/avoid' (built from گریز 'flee/avoid' with prohibitive sense), and بیش 'more'/'any longer' modifies it: 'do not flee any more.'
segments.s671-g127: وزین روی is an adjectival phrase 'with that weighty/dignified face' (وزین = weighty/dignified, روی = face/countenance). خراد here functions as an epithet 'wise' or as part of a name; برزین is a proper name (Barzin). نهان modifies the clause as an adverb 'secretly/hiddenly.' In this poetic register epithets and names can appear adjacent without additional particles.
segments.s671-g128: همی‌تاخت is an imperfect/progressive past verb 'was charging' or 'rushed' (همی- marks continuous action in classical Persian). تا = 'until' introduces the limit of motion, نزد = 'to the presence of' (rather than mere proximity), and شاه جهان is an honorific compound 'king of the world' (شاه = king, جهان = world) used as the referent of the destination.
segments.s671-g105: همی‌راند is an older/progressive past-form meaning “kept riding/was riding.” تا introduces a goal/limit here and means “until.” در دبیر بزرگ is a locative phrase; in classical/poetic usage this line refers to a great entrance/gate (درب بزرگ) or a prominent place — بزرگ is the adjective “great.” Word order is verb → temporal/goal phrase, typical of narrative poetry.
segments.s671-g106: رسید is the simple past “reached/arrived.” و is the coordinating conjunction “and.” برآشفت is a past tense verb meaning “sprang up / became enraged / rushed” (a sudden action). برسان here functions poetically as part of the following verbal action (roughly ‘fell upon / came upon’), with گرگ (wolf) placed after the verb in the typical Persian poetic order (verb before subject).
segments.s671-g107: ازو چیز بستد is an archaic/poetic sequence meaning “took from him a thing / secured from him”; بستد here has the sense of seizing or securing (poetic use of بستن). همه هرچ داشت means “all/whatever he had” (همه + relative هرچ + past داشت “he had”), with the possessed-items phrase following the verb as is common in older Persian.
segments.s671-g108: ببند is an imperative or jussive form meaning “bind / fasten / pack up.” گرانش is a possessive compound meaning “his heavy things / his valuables” (گران = heavy/valuable + -ش = his). ز راه بازگشت is a verb phrase “returned from the road,” where ز means “from,” راه “road/path,” and بازگشت here is the past “(they/he) returned”; subject is omitted but understood from context.
segments.s671-g109: به نزدیک = “to/near” (به + نزدیک) indicating movement toward proximity. بهرام is the proper name. بردش is the past form of برد + object clitic -ش “him” (classical Persian word-order can place the object clitic on the verb): here it means “brought/took him.” ز راه = “from the road/path.” The sequence thus means they brought him up close to Bahram from the road.
segments.s671-g110: بدان is the adverbial/archaic combination (ب + دان) meaning “by that / thereby / to that end.” تاکند is a subjunctive-purpose clause: تا + کنند → “so that they may / in order that they might.” بیگنه را is the object with را marking “the stranger/alien.” تباه is the result “ruined/destroyed.” Put together: “by that, so that they might make the stranger ruined” = “so that they might destroy the stranger.”
segments.s671-g111: بدو گفت بهرام is an instance of flexible classical Persian word order: بدو “to him” (indirect object) + گفت “said” (past), with the speaker بهرام following the verb — i.e. “Bahram said to him.” کای is the vocative particle “O,” and دیوساز is an epithet “demon‑maker / troublemaker.”
segments.s671-g112: چرارفتی is an interrogative past-form “why did you go?” (چرا + رفتی). از پیش من is a locative phrase “from before me / from my presence” (از = from, پیش = in front of/ in the presence of, من = me). بی‌جواز is an adverb/adjective “without leave / unauthorized,” modifying the action and giving the sense “without permission.”
segments.s671-g345: This is a terse epic-style clause giving the speaker and time: ‘بهمدان گشسب’ = “Gashasp of Hamadan” (name with place), followed by the temporal phrase ‘آن زمان’ (“at that time”), and the verb phrase ‘گفت باز’ (“said again”). Word order is subject (place+name) → time adjunct → verb → adverb. ‘‘باز’ here is an adverb modifying گفت meaning “again.”
segments.s671-g346: This is a relative/address clause introduced by ‘که‌ای’ (= ‘who / O you who’), followed by the participle ‘گشته’ (= ‘having become / having been’), and the locative phrase ‘اندر نشیب وفراز’ literally “in descent and ascent” — an idiom for experiencing ups and downs. The participle describes the state of the addressee; ‘نشیب و فراز’ is a fixed antonymic pair meaning ‘downs and ups.’
segments.s671-g347: ‘سخن هرچ‌گویی’ = ‘the speech/words that you say’ (هرچ‌گویی modifies سخن: ‘whatever you say’). The verb ‘بروی’ here means ‘go / reach’ (3rd-person meaning ‘it goes / reaches’). ‘کسان’ is the indefinite plural ‘people’ — the phrase states that whatever is said will reach people.
segments.s671-g348: ‘شود باد’ is an optative formula: شد (become) + باد (optative particle) = “may it be” / “let it be so.” The predicate that follows (‘وکردار او نارسان’) lacks an overt copula — typical in Persian — so نارسان is a predicative adjective describing كردارِ او (“his deed/behavior”). The whole clause expresses a wish or comment that, notwithstanding words reaching people, his deeds are lacking.
segments.s671-g349: Imperative verb ‘بگو’ = ‘say / tell.’ The object is the relative/indefinite clause ‘آنچ دانی’ = ‘that which you know’ (آنچ = ‘what / that which’; دانی = 2nd-singular present of ‘know’). ‘به کار اندورن’ is a prepositional/pertaining phrase ‘regarding the affairs/matters of the inner place’ (به کار = ‘concerning the matter’, اندورن = ‘the inner quarters’).
segments.s671-g350: A short nominal phrase specifying what concerns the inner quarters: ‘زنیک’ (a woman, here likely ‘the (young/beautiful) woman’) joined by ‘و’ to ‘بد روزگار اندرون’ (‘and the ill-fated/evil times within’). The final ‘اندرون’ localizes these items to the inner apartments; the coordination links two topics about the interior.
segments.s671-g351: ‘چنین’ = ‘thus,’ an adverbial framing the speech. ‘گفت’ is the verb ‘said’ (here used as past narrative). The name phrase ‘همدان گشسب بلند’ places the place+name+epithet together: ‘Gashasp of Hamadan, the tall/lofty.’ The epithet ‘بلند’ follows the name as is common in epic phrasing.
segments.s671-g352: This clause begins with ‘که‌ای’ used as a relative or vocative (‘who / O you who’), followed by ‘نزد’ + a noun phrase meaning ‘in the presence of / among.’ ‘پرمایگان ارجمند’ = ‘the eminent / noble great ones’ (an honorific plural). The whole fragment addresses or describes someone ‘who is among the esteemed nobles.’
segments.s671-g265: پس here is a temporal/locative adverb meaning “then/after” and introduces the scene; پرده is the noun “curtain,” so پس پرده functions like “then/behind the curtain.” نامور پهلوان is a noun phrase in which نامور (an epithet) precedes and modifies پهلوان, yielding “the renowned champion.”
segments.s671-g266: یکی ... بود is the classical pattern meaning “one (was) ...” — یکی is the numeral/indefinite “one” and بود is the past copula “was.” خواهرش is خواهر + enclitic ش (“his/her”), i.e. “his sister.” روشن‌روان is a proper epithet/name meaning “bright‑souled.” The clause states that one of his sisters was named Roshan‑ravān.
segments.s671-g267: This is a nominal copular clause in the order subject + name + نام + بود: خردمند (the prudent/wise one) followed by راگردیه (here functioning as the personal name) and then نام بود “was named.” In classical Persian the name can be placed directly after the noun it names.
segments.s671-g268: A short appositive list: دلارام is a proper name, وانجام here functions adverbially as “and finally” (marking the last item), and بهرام is the final name; بود (past copula) at the end applies to the enumerated names. This yields “Dolarām, and finally Bahram.”
segments.s671-g269: چواز is the fused subordinating phrase چُو + از meaning “when/from (the time) that”; پرده is the locus “the curtain.” گفت is a past tense verb “(she) spoke/said” (subject omitted). The main clause follows: برادر شنید (“the brother heard”). Together: “When (she) spoke from the curtain, the brother heard.”
segments.s671-g270: برآشفت is a past intransitive verb meaning “he became upset/angry.” وز here is a classical poetic form equivalent to از (“from/because of”); کین is the cause “hatred/vengeance.” دلش (“his heart”) is the possessed noun with enclitic ش, and بردمید is a perfective verb meaning “seized/took (hold of)” or “leaped” in this idiomatic context. The clause says his heart was seized/moved by hatred.
segments.s671-g271: بران is a connective/adverb meaning “thereupon” or “upon that.” انجمن شد is the light-verb construction meaning “an assembly was held” (انجمن = assembly, شد = occurred/was). سری is an adjective “secret/private,” and پرسخن (پر + سخن fused) describes the assembly as “talkative” or “full of speech.” The phrase describes a private, loquacious council being convened.
segments.s671-g272: زبان here means “speech/tongue” (i.e. manner of speaking). پر is the adjective “full (of),” and the following z (ز) is the poetic preposition “of/from” introducing the complement گفتارهای کهن “ancient sayings/utterances.” The whole unit means “a tongue/speech full of ancient sayings.”
segments.s671-g17: ببین is the imperative 'look/see' (verb-first). تا کجا رفت is a subordinate clause introduced by تا + interrogative کجا meaning 'how far / to what place' with the past verb رفت 'went'. سالار ما (our commander) is the logical subject/agent; Persian often places a past-tense verb before its subject in poetic lines. ما here follows سالار as a possessive 'our'.
segments.s671-g18: This is a nominal apposition elaborating 'سالار ما' — a string of titles/epithets. سپهبد is a title (spahbed/commander), یل is an epithet 'hero', نامبردار is a postpositive adjective 'renowned/celebrated', and ما again marks possession 'our'. The whole group names the same person mentioned before.
segments.s671-g19: This clause uses a compact word order typical of epic poetry. یلان 'the heroes' (plural) is the affected group, سینه 'breasts/chests' is the noun describing them, درکاخ 'in the palace' gives the location, and بنهاد (past tense of نهاد) is the verb 'placed/put' — verb follows the locative phrase here. روی appears as a noun 'face' or 'countenance' used here in close connection with the action (poetic framing of how they were set).
segments.s671-g20: This is a short adjectival/adverbial phrase modifying the action: دلی 'a heart' (here a state), پر ز اندیشه 'full of thought' uses پر + ز + noun to mean 'full of X', and سالار جوی is a compound where جوی (from جستن) functions as 'seeking/searching' so 'yearning for / seeking the commander'. The whole phrase describes the actor's inner state while he acts.
segments.s671-g21: یکی here functions as an indefinite determiner 'a certain / a single', followed by the coordinated nouns طاق و ایوان 'arch and iwan'. فرخنده is an adjective 'auspicious/fortunate' modifying the compound, and دید is the past-tense verb 'he saw' taking the whole phrase as its object. Word order is typical: determiner + noun(s) + adjective + verb.
segments.s671-g22: کزان سان is a relative/correlative phrase 'from which / of such a sort'. به ایران here marks the scope/place ('in Iran' / 'for Iran'), and نه ... دید و شنید is a negative past construction: نه before the verb gives 'neither ... saw nor heard' (a poetic negation of both seeing and hearing). The clause asserts that Iran had neither seen nor heard the like.
segments.s671-g23: نهاده is the past participle/past form 'placed/put' (used as a finite verb here). بایوان is a poetic/compound form (here to be read as 'with iwan' / 'بـ ایوان' = 'with its porch'); او is the possessive 'his/its' linked to the preceding noun, and تخت زر is the object 'golden throne' (تخت 'throne' modified by زر 'gold'). The verb governs the following noun phrase.
segments.s671-g24: نشانده is a causative/resultant participle 'having set/placed' or 'seated', بهر 'for / on / toward' indicates purpose or placement, پایه‌ای here conveys 'on a base / upon a pedestal' (paya-ī), and درگهر 'in the house/hall' gives the location. Together this gives where and how the throne was set.
segments.s671-g217: بیاید is a 3rd-person present/jussive form meaning 'let [them] come' or 'may [they] come'—an optative/imperative-like opening that introduces what should happen. به نزدیک ایرانیان is a prepositional phrase ('toward/near the Iranians') with به + نزدیک (direction/position) and ایرانیان as the target; the phrase specifies where they should come.
segments.s671-g218: ببندند is a 3rd-person plural subjunctive/jussive of بستن used here in the sense 'to close/bring to an end' ('let them close'). پیکار وکین is the compound object 'battle and vengeance/hatred'; را is the definite-object marker attaching to that object, and میان ('between') locates the action as occurring between the parties. Word order is verb (ببندند) + object (پیکار وکین را) + locative (میان).
segments.s671-g219: برین is a fused prepositional phrase (بر + این = 'on/over this'), with نیز ('also') adding 'too' or 'again'. بگذشت is the past tense 'passed' (impersonal use: 'a time passed'), and روزگار is the noun 'time/period' serving as the subject of the impersonal verb. The whole segment means 'a period of time also passed over this.'
segments.s671-g220: نخواندند is the past plural of خواندن with the negative prefix ن- here meaning 'did not read'; placed before کس ('anyone/no one') this yields an existential negative 'no one read.' نامه شهریار is the direct object 'the king's letter' (نامه modified by شهریار). Word order emphasizes the negated action before the agent/indefinite 'کس.'
segments.s671-g221: ازان پس is a temporal sequence: ازان ('from that/thereupon') + پس ('after/then') = 'after that.' گرانمایگان را بخواند: را is the object marker and بخواند here is used in the sense 'to call/summon' (not 'read'); together it means 'he summoned the illustrious ones (the nobles).' The verb is past narrative.
segments.s671-g222: بسی functions as an intensifier 'much/many' (here modifying رازها, 'secrets'). پیش ایشان is a prepositional phrase 'in their presence/before them' (پیش = before/in front of; ایشان = them). براند (from راندن) is the verb meaning 'he set/placed/put (before them)'—here idiomatically 'he revealed/presented many secrets to them.'
segments.s671-g223: چوهمدان here functions as a name/epithet ('(of) Hamadan' / 'Hamadan's') attached to گشسب (the hero Gushasp); و (contracted to و‑) دبیر بزرگ = 'and the great scribe/secretary' is a coordinated noun phrase. The sequence lists important personages called to the assembly: 'Gushasp of Hamadan and the great secretary.'
segments.s671-g224: یلان سینه is a classical epithet: یلان (plural of یل) 'heroes' governed by سینه ('of the chest/heart' = 'valiant-hearted'). آن نامدار سترگ is a descriptive tag modifying them: آن ('that/the') + نامدار ('renowned') + سترگ (an archaic epithet meaning lofty/mighty). Together the segment praises these figures as 'the valorous-hearted, that famed and mighty (ones).' Word order is noun phrase + adjectival qualifiers.
segments.s671-g305: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. پیروزی و فرهی are coordinated noun phrases ('victory and honour') placed before the verb in typical SOV order. یابد is a third-person present/optative verb 'attains/receives' that governs both nouns, and اوی is the archaic/poetic third-person pronoun 'he' placed after the verb for emphasis.
segments.s671-g306: بسوی بدی is a prepositional phrase 'toward evil' placed before the verb. هیچ‌نشتابد is a negated present-form meaning 'does not hurry/does not rush (at all)', with هیچ adding strong negation/emphasis. اوی again is the emphatic/archaic third-person pronoun 'he' following the verb.
segments.s671-g307: که introduces a subordinate/result clause 'that/so that'. آن آفرین (literally 'that praise/acclamation') is the noun phrase; باز is the adverb 'again' modifying the following noun/phrase; نفرین شود is a result/passive construction 'become a curse' (شود = 'become' in subjunctive/result sense). Word order places the noun phrase and adverb before the impersonal/passive verb.
segments.s671-g308: وزو is an archaic/source phrase indicating 'from him' (here marking origin/source of the following effect). چرخ گردنده is a compound 'the turning/revolving wheel' (a common fate/metaphor); گردنده is a participial/adjectival form 'turning/rotating'. پرکین شود is a result clause with شود 'becomes'—the meaning of پرکین is irregular/rare here and so the line expresses that the turning wheel (of fate) will change/become adverse with respect to him.
segments.s671-g309: چو begins a temporal clause 'when'. یزدان is the subject 'God'. تو را is the object marked with را ('to you'); فرهی داد is 'gave honour' (داد is past 'gave') and the coordination with و links بخت so that the verb داد is understood to apply to both 'فرهی' and 'بخت' ('honour and fortune'). Word order places the indirect object and the given goods before the past verb, typical of Persian poetic style.
segments.s671-g310: همه لشکر گنج lists items that accompany the bestowed fortune: 'the whole host, (the) treasure'. با is the preposition 'with', introducing the possessions that go with it (تاج و تخت 'crown and throne'). The phrase is a nominal list functioning as the complement/extension of 'fortune' previously mentioned.
segments.s671-g311: ازو‌گر is an archaic conditional phrase ('if from him' / 'if it is from him'); پذیری is a second-person singular present/subjunctive 'you accept' or a generic 'one accepts'. بافزون شود is a verbal phrase 'becomes increased/it will increase' (بافزون 'increased/more' + شود 'become'), so the clause means that accepting from him will make it increase.
segments.s671-g312: دل 'the heart' is the subject. از ناسپاسی is an ablative phrase 'from/through ingratitude' giving the cause, and پرازخون (پر + از + خون) is an adjectival phrase 'full of blood' or 'blood-stained'. شود is the resultative 'becomes' — together: 'the heart, because of ingratitude, becomes full of blood.'
segments.s671-g113: چنین (thus) is an adverbial introducer setting the manner of the reply. داد (gave) is a past tense verb meaning 'gave' and together with پاسخ (answer) forms the collocation 'gave an answer'; که introduces the reported speech that follows, and ای پهلوان is a vocative address ('O champion'). Word order is classical: manner + verb + noun (داد پاسخ) then a complementizer + vocative.
segments.s671-g114: مراکرد is a compact past construction 'mara kard' = 'made me' (object-pronoun + past verb). خراد here functions as an adjective/noun meaning 'wise/wisdom' in the phrase 'made me wise'. برزین نوان is a proper name (a compound epithet: Barzin-nūān), appearing after the verb as the logical agent (‘Barzin-nūān made me wise’). This reflects the flexible word order of Classical Persian where the subject/agent can follow the verb.
segments.s671-g115: همی‌گفت is an iterative/habitual past form meaning 'he kept saying' or 'he would say' (the prefix همی marks ongoing/habitual aspect). کایدر is an older/dialectal locative/adverb ('where' / 'here' in context) introducing the locative comment; بدن روی نیست is a verbless/predicative clause with نیست (is not) negating 'body's face' (i.e. 'there is no face on the body' — a literal reading; poetically it can mean 'no honor/face remains on the body').
segments.s671-g116: درنگ تو is a noun phrase 'your delay/hesitation' (درنگ = delay/tarry, تو = you). جز introduces an exclusion/comparison 'except; nothing but', followed by کام (desire/wish) and بدگوی (literally 'ill-speaker' or 'ill-speaking' — here an abstract verbal noun meaning 'speaking ill / slandering'). نیست (is not) negates the clause: 'your delay is nothing but a wish to slander.' The structure is standard Persian: topic (درنگ تو) + predicate (جز ... نیست).
segments.s671-g117: مرا و تو را are the two definite/accusative objects (مرا = me, تو = you) each marked by را (object marker). بیم is a nominal predicate 'fear/danger' and کشتن is the infinitive 'to kill' functioning as the complement of بیم ('fear of killing'). بود is the past-tense copula/existential 'was' — together giving 'there was a fear of killing me and you.' segments.s671-g215: پراگند is a poetic adjective/adverb meaning ‘scattered / dispersed’ used here as an instruction ‘scatter / station’. بر گرد = ‘around/about’ (prepositional phrase), کشور = ‘the country/realm’, and سوار = ‘horsemen / mounted men’ (either noun or adjectival ‘mounted’). segments.s671-g97: The phrase is adjective + noun + prepositional phrase + past plural verb. چورنگ (an adjective) pre-modifies گریز (here a noun = 'flight/fugitives'). اندر is a poetic preposition meaning 'in/among', and آمیختند is the 3rd-person plural past of آمیختن ('to mix/mingle'). segments.s671-g99: سپهبد is the subject ('the spahbed'). چو here means 'when' and introduces a temporal subordinate clause. آگه شد is a light verbal phrase: آگه 'aware' + شد 'became' = 'became aware of'. ازکارشان (از + کار + شان) marks the matter or action he became aware of ('of their deed/work').

segments.s671-g102: بتاز is an imperative/jussive form (ب‑ prefix + تَاز) meaning 'gallop!/rush!/charge!'. از پس is a prepositional pair meaning 'after' or 'in pursuit of'. این دو is 'these two' (direct object), and ناهوشیار is an adjective 'unaware/heedless' modifying those two. segments.s671-g103: بیامد is the simple past 3rd‑person singular 'he came'. از آنجا is the source phrase 'from there'. بکردار is a poetic adjective/compound (ب‑ + کردار) meaning 'of good conduct/with a noble bearing', and گرد here closes the phrase: together بکردار گرد functions as a predicative/qualifying expression ('having assumed a noble bearing' or 'of good conduct'). segments.s671-g41: همانگه is an adverbial temporal phrase 'at that moment' placed up front for narrative tempo. پرستنده‌گان را marks the people being affected with the object marker را after the plural noun (worshippers). به راه is a goal phrase 'onto the road' with به + noun indicating motion/towards.

segments.s671-g44: پرآگند is an adjectival/participial description 'full / filled'. زینها 'the saddles' follows the adjective (classical word order). همه بشمرند is a verbal clause: بشمرند is a narrative/formal 3rd‑person plural form meaning 'they counted' (older narrative morphology where -ند appears in reported past).
segments.s671-g45: درباغ is a locative phrase 'in/at the garden'. بگشاد here functions as a past/actionive sense 'opened' (classical wording rather than the purely adjectival meaning). پالیزبان is the agent 'the gardener'; word order places the location first, then the verb form and the agent.

segments.s671-g48: بباغ 'to/into the garden' marks direction of motion. از پی is a serial phrase 'after/in pursuit / following' describing his approach; واژ here (literally 'overturned') is used poetically to mean 'turned/fell over' (i.e. prostrated or turned his body), and برسم بدست is a verbal compound where برسم (from رسیدن) means 'to reach/arrive at' and بدست 'by/into the presence' completes the motion toward the addressee. segments.s671-g297: چوخشنود is a poetic/adverbial description meaning “very pleased”; باشد is the copular verb in a subjunctive/optative sense here (“be/is”); ز is the archaic/prepositional form meaning “from/of” used poetically (z + pronoun = “with/from me”); من is the first-person pronoun; پهلوان is the predicate noun (“the hero/champion”). segments.s671-g298: برآنم is an idiom meaning “I am resolved/determined”; که introduces the subordinate clause “that …”; جاوید is an adjective ‘eternal’; مانم is the 1st‑person singular present/subjunctive of ماندن (‘I remain / may I remain’) indicating the speaker’s wish/resolve; جوان is an adjective ‘young / youthful’ placed after جاوید for poetic emphasis: “I am resolved that I shall remain forever young.”
segments.s671-g299: چوبهرام here is the poetic contraction of چو بهرام = 'when Bahram'; بشنید is the simple past 3rd‑person 'he heard'; گفتار اوی is a genitive phrase 'his speech/words' (اوی = his). The clause reads: 'When Bahram heard his words...'.
segments.s671-g300: می‌انجی is a locative/nominal noun here meaning ‘in the assembly/gathering’ (poetic form); همی‌دید uses the prefix همی + past stem to give an iterative/continuous past sense ('he perceived/kept seeing'); کردار اوی = 'his deed/behavior' (اوی = his), the object observed in the assembly. segments.s671-g303: یلان سینه گفت is a formulaic phrase 'the heroes (speaking) from their breasts/hearts said' (سینه indicating sincerity). ای is the vocative particle 'O'; سپهدار is the addressee's title 'commander'; گرد is an archaic verb/imperative here meaning 'turn/come near' or a call to attention—used poetically as a vocative accompaniment. segments.s671-g185: فرستاد is a past tense verb 'he sent/dispatched' used transitively; here it introduces an action whose object is expressed after it. وایرانیان را uses the object marker را to mark 'the Iranians' as the definite direct object of فرستاد. بخواند is an infinitive/purpose clause meaning 'to call/summon' (here functioning as the intended action of what was sent), so the whole sequence means 'he sent (for) the Iranians, to summon them.'
segments.s671-g186: همه گرد = 'all around' (همه = everyone; گرد = around). آن سله is a definite noun phrase 'that gift/recompense' (آن = that; سله = gift). اندرنشاند is a past transitive verb meaning 'caused to sit / seated (them) inside/around' (compound verb with اندر- prefixed). segments.s671-g189: پراندیشه شد is a copular construction: پراندیشه ('full of thought', i.e. anxious/worried) + شد 'became', so 'became anxious.' لشکر is the subject 'the army/host.' ازکار شاه is a prepositional/construct phrase 'about/regarding the king's affairs' (ازکار here conveys concerning/by-reason-of in classical usage).

segments.s671-g73: دگر روز (literally 'other day') here means 'the next day' (دگر = another/next). چون is used in the temporal sense 'when/as', followed by سیمگون گشت, a predicative clause: سیمگون (silvery) + گشت (past of گشتن 'to become') = 'became silvery'. راغ is an archaic/poetic word whose sense is uncertain in the provided list and here functions as a temporal/nominal element tied to the dawn/day; it links directly to the following clause which reports what then happened.
segments.s671-g74: پدید آمد is a verb phrase meaning 'appeared' (پدید = appear; آمد = came) with the verb before the demonstrative phrase (verb first is common in poetic prose). آن is the demonstrative 'that/the', and the noun phrase زرد رخشان چراغ combines two adjectival modifiers (زرد 'yellow', رخشان 'radiant') with چراغ 'lamp/light' to yield 'that yellow, radiant lamp.'
segments.s671-g75: بگسترد is a past/transitive verb 'spread/laid out' (ب‑ prefix is a stylistic/past marker here). فرشی is the noun 'a carpet' (فرشی used as the object), and ز دیبای چین is a prepositional phrase 'from/of the brocade of China' (ز = 'from/of', دیبای = 'brocade/silk', چین = 'China' here). segments.s671-g76: تو گفتی is a reporting clause 'you said' (تو = 'you'); the gloss for گفتی in the provided list did not include the verb sense 'you said' so its sense index is marked -1. مگر is a rhetorical particle here ('is it that/perhaps'), and آسمان شد زمین is a verbless-turned-verbal phrase with شد 'became' expressing 'the sky became the earth.' segments.s671-g1: ازان دشت (azān dasht) is a prepositional fronting: 'from that plain'. بهرام یل is the subject with a noun in apposition (بهرام, the name, plus یل 'hero'). بنگرید is an archaic/past-3rd-person form of نگریدن used in epic poetry meaning 'he looked' or 'beheld'.
segments.s671-g2: یکی is an indefinite 'a certain' (not the numeral here). کاخ پرمایه = 'a substantial/rich palace' (پرمایه modifying کاخ). آمد پدید is a poetic verb pair meaning 'came into sight/appeared' (آمد + پدید, with the order slightly inverted for verse).
segments.s671-g3: بران ('on that' / 'thereupon') marks the location. کاخ بنهاد is a past action 'he set up/established a palace' (بنهاد = founded/placed). The name بهرام is the doer (subject) placed after the verb in the epic word order. روی here means 'with its face toward' (i.e. 'facing').
segments.s671-g4: همان گور = 'that same grave'. پیش = 'before, in front of'. اندرون راه جوی is a noun phrase 'the inner way / inner waterway' (اندرون 'inner' modifies راه 'way', with جوی 'stream/watercourse' specifying the kind of way). segments.s671-g5: همی‌راند is an imperfective/habitual past form 'he kept driving/was riding'. تا introduces the goal 'until', and پیش آن کاخ gives the endpoint 'before that palace'. اسب is the object 'the horse' of راندن (he was riding the horse); its position after the locative phrase is a poetic inversion.
segments.s671-g6: پس is a connective 'then/thereupon'. پشت او = 'at his back/behind him' (پشت + pronoun او). بود is the past copula 'was'. ایزد here is an honorific/epithet 'divine' or 'godly' applied to the name گشسب (Gushasp), the hero who is thus presented as a lofty figure.
segments.s671-g7: عنان تگاور بدو داد = 'he gave the reins to the rider'. عنان 'reins' is the direct object, تگاور 'the rider/warrior' is in apposition or specifies whose reins, بدو 'to him' is the indirect object, and داد is the past 'gave'. وگفت simply links the speech 'and said'.
segments.s671-g8: که introduces the reported wish/clause. با تو 'with you' = 'accompanying you'. همیشه 'always'. خرد 'wisdom'. باد is the optative particle meaning 'may (it be)'. جفت here means 'companion/partner' (literally 'pair'), so the phrase reads 'may wisdom always be your companion.'
segments.s671-g225: This is a temporal subordinate chunk headed by چو (here attached to the name), i.e. 'چو بهرام' = 'when Bahram...'. گرد functions as a local/adverbial particle 'around/near' governing the following noun phrase 'آن سیاوش نژاد' ('that one of Siavash's line'). segments.s671-g226: چوپیدا = چو پیدا, an archaic compound meaning 'when ... was found / when ... appeared'. گشسب is a proper name (Gushasp) and is followed by the appositive آن خردمند ('that wise one'). وراد is an older spelling of وارد, used here as a past/participial idea 'entered/arrived' (i.e. 'appeared'). segments.s671-g227: همی is a durative/progressive marker (used with past tense verbs to mean 'kept on / continually'). رای زد is an idiomatic verb phrase: رای 'counsel/plan' + زدن (here 'زد') = 'to give counsel / express an opinion'. با چنین مهتران = 'with such chiefs/leaders' (چنین = 'such'; مهتران is an older plural noun 'chiefs').
segments.s671-g228: که introduces a relative clause 'who'. بودند is the past plural of بودن 'they were'. شیران is literal 'lions' but used metaphorically here = 'brave men/valiant champions'. کنداوران is an epithet (literally 'those who dig/excavate') and functions as a group-name/attribute in apposition; its exact connotation is idiomatic/poetic.
segments.s671-g229: چنین = 'thus'. گفت is the past-tense verb 'he said/spoke' (narrative report). پس = 'then' (sequence). پهلوان سپاه is a genitive nominal group 'the champion of the army' (پهلوان followed by the possessor noun سپاه). The order reflects classical Persian narrative style.
segments.s671-g230: بدان = به + آن = 'to/for that'. لشکر is 'the host/army' and is the referent of بدان. تیزگم is an adjectival compound meaning 'swift' (modifying an implied motion or the following noun). کرده is a perfect/participle 'having made' and راه is 'road/way' — together 'having made a swift road for that army' (participial clause placed before its noun, a common classical construction).
segments.s671-g231: که‌ای functions as a vocative/exclamatory address ('O ...'). نامداران is the plural 'the famed/renowned ones'. گردن‌فراز is an epithet placed after the noun meaning 'proud / high‑necked' (a conventional heroic epithet). The whole is an address to the distinguished men present.
segments.s671-g232: برای شما = 'for you' (benefactive). هرکسی = 'whomever / anyone' (indefinite object). را is the definite/object marker that highlights the object. نیاز here functions as 'need / require' (older usage can be verbal — 'I need/ask for'); together: 'I need/ask for anyone for you' (i.e. 'I will provide someone for you').
segments.s671-g145: Word order is poetic: modifier + subject + object marker + verb. سبک modifies موبد (an epithet attached to the priest), موبد موبدان is the subject phrase naming the priestly rank, را is the definite-object marker attaching to موبدان, and بخواند is a past third-person verb meaning 'called/summoned' here (classical b‑prefix + past).
segments.s671-g146: بران جای is a locative phrase 'on/at that place'; خراد برزین is a proper-name phrase (خراد as name element 'wise' + برزین); نشاند is an archaic past verb meaning 'set/placed/seat(ed)'. The clause thus says someone seated Kharad Barzin at that place.
segments.s671-g147: The line starts with an address 'ب خراد برزین' (to Kharad Barzin) followed by the report 'چنین گفت شاه' = 'thus said the king'; word order is inverted for poetic effect (indirect object / address first, then the verb + subject). Several lexical items are presented in forms that here function as a vocative/indirect-address construction.
segments.s671-g148: که introduces the content of the king's speech. بگشای is an imperative/optative form 'open' (classical b‑form of the verb), لب ('lip') is used metonymically for 'speak', تا is 'so that/in order that' introducing the subordinate clause, چه دیدی is a past interrogative 'what did you see' (دیدی = 2nd‑person past), and به راه is the prepositional phrase 'on/along the road.'
segments.s671-g149: بفرمان هرمز is an agentive/prepositional phrase 'by the command of Hormoz' (بـ + فرمان). زبان is the grammatical subject 'tongue/speech', and برگشاد is a past form 'opened' (classical perfective), so the clause literally says 'by Hormoz's command the tongue opened.'
segments.s671-g150: سخنها 'the words/speeches' is the theme, یکایک 'one by one' modifies it, همه reinforces 'all', and کرد یاد is a verbal phrase where کرد (to do/make) combines with یاد (remembrance/mention) to mean 'recalled' or 'related/mentioned.' The sequence therefore expresses that he recounted every word one by one.
segments.s671-g151: بدوشاه is a prepositional/address phrase 'to the king' or 'before the king' and introduces reported speech; گفت is used as the reporting verb 'said' (here the supplied sense list does not include the verbal sense), این چه 'what is this' is an interrogative formula, and شاید is the modal 'perhaps'. The item بدن in this position is ambiguous in the provided lemma list; contextually it functions as a connective/particle linking to the following clause (see note).
segments.s671-g152: The clause has the modal phrase بباید 'it must/one ought' followed by the infinitive زدن; همه داستانها is the logical subject/object 'all the tales/stories.' In classical usage زدن here is used in the sense 'to relate/record/tell' (cf. 'to write/record' in the listed senses), so the line says that perhaps all the stories must be told/recorded.
